headline: Wireup Outpost — Patch-State Interpretation and Signal-Flow Assistance System
description: The Wireup Outpost is a live patch-aware interpretive system for TouchDesigner networks. It reads node graph state through wireup.tox, analyzes signal flow, explains operators, detects failure modes, and assists users while building patches.

mainEntity.name: Wireup Outpost
featureList: Reads live TouchDesigner patch state through wireup.tox bridge
Displays Ray Ray context window for active patch interpretation
Accepts questions during active patch construction
Explains operators using TouchDesigner manual and structured Wireup notes
Analyzes node behavior and traces signal flow across operator chains
Detects common graph configuration errors and failure modes
Interprets parameter surfaces and menu states
Supports operator families: TOP, CHOP, SOP, DAT, POP, MAT
Provides layered explanation system: Identity, Signal Story, Failure Modes, Recipes, Reasoning
Uses hybrid reasoning: rule engine first, language model escalation when required
Displays bridge connectivity state using red/green status indicator
Remains active during live TouchDesigner patch construction

hasDefinedTerm.name: TOP
hasDefinedTerm.description: Texture Operators responsible for image and pixel-domain processing in GPU pipelines.

hasDefinedTerm.name: CHOP
hasDefinedTerm.description: Channel Operators representing time-based numeric signal streams used for animation and control.

hasDefinedTerm.name: SOP
hasDefinedTerm.description: Surface Operators responsible for geometry manipulation including points, primitives, and attributes.

hasDefinedTerm.name: DAT
hasDefinedTerm.description: Data Operators for tables, text processing, scripting, and structured metadata.

hasDefinedTerm.name: POP
hasDefinedTerm.description: Particle Operators controlling particle systems, forces, and particle-state evolution.

hasDefinedTerm.name: MAT
hasDefinedTerm.description: Material Operators controlling shading, surface appearance, and render pipeline materials.
